Sample is small but the systematic underestimate magnitude matches the other 4 pitchers, so this row is on the same systemic-bias line, not a small-sample artifact.

root_cause_diagnosis.summary: Boxscore `pitching.flyOuts` is OUTS RECORDED on flyballs, not TOTAL FLYBALLS HIT. xFIP requires total flyballs (FB% × balls-in-play, plus home runs since HRs are flyballs). FanGraphs computes FB from Statcast batted-ball event data: `FB = FB% × BIP + HR`.
root_cause_diagnosis.evidence: Burnes 2024 published HR/FB% on FG = 12.4% with 22 HR. Implied total FB ≈ 22 / 0.124 ≈ 177. Boxscore flyOuts sum across 32 starts = 94. Boxscore captures ~53% of FG-FB universe — the missing ~47% is hits-on-flyballs that didn't produce outs (singles/doubles/triples on flyballs) plus the HRs themselves. Across all 5 pitchers, computed xFIP underestimates by 0.64-0.81 — a systematic offset consistent with FB universe being roughly half the correct value.
what_NOT_the_problem: Constants table — TS and Python parity confirms transcription is internally consistent. Even if LG_HR_PER_FB were doubled to ~0.23 to compensate, the formula would only match for a single season; the cross-season pattern would still be off.
Formula transcription — formula matches FG canonical xFIP form exactly: ((13 * FB * lgHRperFB) + 3*(BB+HBP) - 2*K) / IP + xFIP_const.
IP convention — already converted from MLB .1/.2 encoding to decimal thirds.
Sample window — full-season aggregates used; not a window-edge artifact.
root_cause_diagnosis.what_IS_the_problem: Input data source mismatch. Boxscore flyOuts ≠ FG-published flyballs. The MLB Stats API boxscore does not expose total batted-ball-event-typed flyballs; that data lives in Statcast pitch-by-pitch / batted-ball-event ingestion, which is a separate (out-of-scope this proposal) data path. The proposal's underlying assumption that boxscore flyOuts gives FG-parity FB count is FALSE.

available_remediation_paths_for_next_res: Path 1 (Statcast batted-ball-event ingestion): Add per-pitch / per-PA batted_ball_events table sourced from Baseball Savant Statcast. Compute FB per appearance from event type = 'fly_ball' + 'home_run' (FG includes HR in FB). Multi-week effort; storage ~10-20 GB for 2022-2024; new ingestion cron. EXPLICITLY out of scope for the current one-holdout-consumption cap per CSO 2026-05-04 verdict.

Path 2 (Substitute proxy from boxscore): Compute FB% from boxscore as flyOuts / (flyOuts + groundOuts + popOuts + lineOuts), apply to (BIP from boxscore) + HR. BIP = AB - K - HR. Approximate but uses only data already in the boxscore. Per-spot-check refit needed; may close the gap to within ±0.20 if the FB%-from-outs proxy correlates well enough with FG's FB%-from-batted-ball-events. Worth a focused experiment as a follow-up research memo.

Path 3 (Use sabermetrics-endpoint xfip directly, season-aggregate with prior-year carry): Same gotcha as wRC+ memo's Path A — endpoint ignores byDateRange; serving would use full-season-as-of-last-completed-season value. Smaller in-season-as-of-date signal but FG-parity by definition. Re-research as a kind:feature-change proposal.

Path 4 (Drop xFIP entirely and pursue a different residual class): TTOP, handedness park splits, lineup composition. Per CSO's `wrcplus-ingestion-revB-2026-05-04-pause` verdict, the holdout-consumption cap allows ONE more pitcher-quality residual attempt before declaring a fresh holdout slice.
